justification: All pass criteria met: 7 failure modes identified (cascading failure, silent notification loss, duplicate charges, timeout budget misalignment, retry storm amplification, Stripe rate limiting, observability gap). Phantom charge risk explicitly addressed as 'duplicate payment charges' with idempotency key mitigation and as 'timeout budget misalignment' where Order gives up while Payment still processes. Fire-and-forget notification weakness thoroughly addressed with transactional outbox pattern. Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific parameters. Idempotency keys specified end-to-end for payment retries. Trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged across three detailed trade-off entries.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failure mode enumeration — 7 distinct modes including subtle ones like retry storm amplification and timeout budget misalignment
Phantom charge risk addressed from two angles: idempotency keys for retry deduplication AND timeout budget hierarchy to prevent the caller-gives-up-while-callee-succeeds scenario
Fire-and-forget replacement with transactional outbox is the correct pattern, and the explanation of why (same-transaction write guarantees) is precise
Critical insight that retries should only happen at one layer in a synchronous chain — prevents multiplicative retry storms
Specific timeout values provided (5s/8s/12s) with explicit budget hierarchy rationale, plus concrete circuit breaker parameters (5 failures in 30s, 15s half-open)
Phased implementation guidance with clear prioritization — idempotency keys first, then circuit breakers, then outbox
Trade-offs are genuinely balanced — acknowledges that keeping sync payment coupling is intentionally rigid and explains why async migration is premature

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"This architecture has three critical integration points with distinct failure characteristics. The synchronous chain from Order→Payment→Stripe creates a cascading failure risk where any downstream outage blocks order processing entirely. The fire-and-forget notification call silently loses emails with no recovery path. The design needs circuit breakers, retries with idempotency, a transactional outbox for notifications, and timeout budgets to prevent cascade amplification."
